title: Neural Tube Defects: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Neural Tube Defects
Identify key diagnostic features of Neural Tube Defects
Apply clinical reasoning to Neural Tube Defects management
Recognize common pitfalls in Neural Tube Defects

sections.title: Overview of Neural Tube Defects
sections.content:
**Neural Tube Defects**

Primary neurulation, failure of closure, anencephaly, spina bifida spectrum, folate prevention

**Key Points:**
• Neural tube closes by day 28 (anterior neuropore day 25, posterior neuropore day 28)
• Anencephaly: failure of anterior neuropore closure → absent cerebral hemispheres (lethal)
• Spina bifida occulta: vertebral arch defect only, skin-covered, usually asymptomatic (tuft of hair, dimple)

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Neural tube closes by day 28 (anterior neuropore day 25, posterior neuropore day 28)
• Anencephaly: failure of anterior neuropore closure → absent cerebral hemispheres (lethal)
• Spina bifida occulta: vertebral arch defect only, skin-covered, usually asymptomatic (tuft of hair, dimple)

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Meningocele: meninges herniate through defect (good prognosis)
• Myelomeningocele: spinal cord + meninges herniate (most common clinically significant NTD), motor/sensory deficits below level
• Prevention: folic acid 400mcg daily (4mg if prior NTD), started BEFORE conception

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Neural Tube Defects:**

• Meningocele: meninges herniate through defect (good prognosis)
• Myelomeningocele: spinal cord + meninges herniate (most common clinically significant NTD), motor/sensory deficits below level
• Prevention: folic acid 400mcg daily (4mg if prior NTD), started BEFORE conception

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of Neural Tube Defects?

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases
Neural tube closes by day 28 (anterior neuropore day 25, posterior neuropore day 28)
options.explanation: Neural tube closes by day 28 (anterior neuropore day 25, posterior neuropore day 28)

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Neural tube closes by day 28 (anterior neuropore day 25, posterior neuropore day 28).
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Neural tube closes by day 28 (anterior neuropore day 25, posterior neuropore day 28)

coachDx.misconception: While typically self-resolving without treatment may seem plausible, it does not match the clinical scenario described.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Neural tube closes by day 28 (anterior neuropore day 25, posterior neuropore day from Typically self-resolving without treatment.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Neural tube closes by day 28 (anterior neuropore day 25, posterior neuropore day 28).
